data.title: SupplyHouse Expands Ohio Fulfillment Center to Strengthen Speed, Capacity and Service Across the Midwest
data.description: Today, in Melville, NY, on March 12, 2026, SupplyHouse, a prominent online retailer of plumbing, HVAC, and electrical supplies, revealed the relocation and expansion of its Ohio fulfillment center. The fulfillment center is moving from Columbus to a much larger facility in Winchester, a suburb of Columbus. This move signifies a substantial growth opportunity for the company in the region.

data.title: Visit West Hollywood Launches "Unmistakably West Hollywood" Campaign and Debuts Reimagined VisitWestHollywood.com
data.description: West Hollywood, California on March 12, 2026 - Visit West Hollywood, the official tourism board, has launched a new campaign called Unmistakably West Hollywood. This campaign aims to showcase the unique experiences available in West Hollywood's renowned 1.9 square mile area.

data.title: TEAMSTERS: WITHOUT WORKER PROTECTIONS, DOJ MUST BLOCK PARAMOUNT-WARNER MERGER
data.description: The International Brotherhood of Teamsters has issued a warning about the potential risks associated with the consolidation of Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. In a statement to the Department of Justice on March 12, 2026, the union expressed concerns that the merger could lead to layoffs, jeopardize industry stability, and undermine labor standards. The union emphasized the importance of considering these factors, particularly given the significant impact the merger could have on the workforce and the broader industry.

data.title: CORRECTING and REPLACING BHMI Enhances the Concourse Financial Software Suite® with Expanded Debit Processing Capabilities
data.description: Omaha, Nebraska - BHMI has recently made significant improvements to its Concourse Financial Software Suite®, a popular back-office payment software solution. The enhancements focus on expanding debit processing capabilities specifically for the Canadian market. As a prominent provider in this industry, BHMI's updates aim to better support debit processing in Canada. This move comes as part of the company's ongoing commitment to refining its software suite.

data.title: Flagstaff packed with festivals celebrating Route 66, Dark Skies, Pride and more
data.description: Flagstaff, Arizona, announced on March 12, 2026, that the high-elevation mountain town, situated near the Grand Canyon, is set to host numerous festivals in 2026. These events will showcase various arts, music, cultural performances, and more. Notable highlights for this year will include the celebration of the Centennial of Route 66 and the 30th edition of Flagstaff Pride. Visitors and residents can look forward to engaging in these festivities throughout the year.

data.title: Totem Surpasses 50,000 Users as Demand for Offline Friend-Finding Technology Grows
data.description: A tech company based in Chattanooga, Tennessee, Totem, unveiled significant achievements related to their friend-finding device, the Totem Compass. With their community exceeding 50,000 users globally, the 2-year old startup is experiencing remarkable expansion in response to the increasing global interest in off-grid technology that operates universally. The Totem Compass, designed for festivals and outdoor escapades, has garnered viral attention and is gaining traction for its ability to function independently in any location.

data.title: Backcast Partners Supports Sisu Energy and Environmental
data.description: A recent announcement from Backcast Partners reveals their latest investment in Sisu Energy & Environmental, LLC, where they provided debt capital to aid in the company's growth and expansion endeavors. Backcast is a prominent provider in the industry, specializing in offering debt and equity capital for middle-market companies. This move signifies their commitment to supporting businesses, reflecting their ongoing efforts to assist companies in achieving their objectives. The transaction showcases Backcast's dedication to facilitating growth opportunities, with the investment aimed at bolstering Sisu Energy & Environmental, LLC and driving its development.

data.title: AdVini and Cordier By InVivo, wich entered exclusive negociations on december 15, sign a binding agreement
data.description: On December 15, 2025, AdVini (Euronext Growth – FR0000053043) and Cordier by InVivo revealed their plan to enhance their presence in the wine industry by initiating exclusive talks. Now, the two companies have confirmed the signing of a definitive agreement for the acquisition where AdVini will acquire a portion of Cordier by InVivo's operations.

data.title: Resume.org Survey: 6 in 10 Hiring Managers Say Creative Thinkers Are More Valuable Than Coders in the Age of AI
data.description: A recent survey conducted in March 2026 by Resume.org, a prominent resume-building platform, of 991 U.S. hiring managers from KIRKLAND, Wash., discovered a growing emphasis on creativity, communication, and storytelling skills as AI continues to automate technical tasks in the workplace. This shift in priorities highlights the importance of soft skills in the job market, with employers placing increasing value on these attributes.

data.title: In HelloNation, Pizza Experts Kelly and Brian Glynn of Columbia Share Tips on Campus Catering
data.description: In Columbia, South Carolina on March 12, 2026, a PRNewswire article discusses the essential steps for organizing a campus pizza event. Highlighting insights from Pizza Experts Kelly and Brian Glynn from Village Idiot Pizza, located in Columbia, SC, the article emphasizes the significance of meticulous planning and coordination in ensuring the event's success. The feature delves into the strategies recommended by the experts, advising on various aspects of event planning to optimize the outcome.

data.title: Karl Studer and Jesse Jensen’s 3 String Cattle Co. Sets Record at Annual Bull Sale, Topping $1.25 Million in Total Gross Revenue
data.description: In Rupert, Idaho, 3 String Cattle Co., led by Karl Studer and Jesse Jensen, recently completed a successful annual bull sale that saw unprecedented outcomes. The event attracted a total of 72 buyers from various parts of the western U.S. and achieved a remarkable total gross revenue of $1,255,000. The auction, which took place at the company's ranch in southern Idaho, had 55 buyers attending in person while 17 joined online. Buyers came from states including Idaho, Utah, Colorado, Nevada, Washington, Oregon, Montana, Texas, and New Mexico.
